english: 1. the consonant y, pronounced like the English initial y, in yard, yoke etc., in C. deep-toned; yá-btags (ya btags), yá-ta (ya ta) Glr. the subscribed y or ྱ. — 2. num. fig.: 24.

I. often with ẏcig (gcig), one of two things that belong together as being of one kind, or forming a pair, also one of two opponents; mig (mig) ya-ẏcig (ya gcig) lóṅ-ba (long ba) Pth. blind of one eye; lham (lham) ya-ẏcig (ya gcig) Glr. one of a pair of boots, an odd boot; lag-pa (lag pa) ya-ẏcig-tu (ya gcig tu) ẏser (gser) togs (thogs), lág-pa (lag pa) ya-ẏcig-tu (ya gcig tu) bú-mo (bu mo) ḱrid-de (khrid de) Dzl. in one hand holding the gold, with the other leading his daughter; stóṅ-pa (stong pa) daṅ (dang) ẏsál-ba (gsal ba) yñis (gnyis) ya (ya) ma (ma) brál-bar (bral bar) Thgr. the empty and the clear (emptiness and clearness) being inseparable from each other; ya-gyál (ya gyal) one of several, e.g. of three things Gram.; of six Lex.; *yá-do* (*ya do*) in W. the common word for grogs (grogs) or zlá-bo (zla bo) associate, companion, assistant, *yá-do (ya do) ċó-ċe* (*co ce*) to assist; *nyi-ka (nyi ka) ya (ya) yo* (*yo*) C. they are equal to each other, a match, one as good as the other, *Kó-la (kho la) ya (ya) mé* (*me*), or kọ (ko) ya (ya) hẹ́-ken (he ken) mi (mi) dug* (dug) C. he finds none that is a match to him, *di (di) lẹ́-ke (le ke) ya (ya) ṅe (nge) mi (mi) tub* (thub) C. I am not equal to the task; Ká-ya (kha ya) v. Ká-ya (kha ya); ya-méd (ya med) = do-méd (do med); ṭáb-ya (thab ya) adversary, antagonist; ya-żár (ya zhar) one-eyed; ya-ma-zúṅ (ya ma zung) and ya-má-brla (ya ma brla), ya-ya (ya ya) v. below. — II. root signifying above, up etc. (opp. to ma (ma)), cf. goṅ (gong); adj. yá-gi (ya gi) (also yá-ki (ya ki) Mil.), p'u (phu) yá-gi (ya gi) the upper or highest part of a valley Glr., ri-bo (ri bo) yá-gi (ya gi) the hill up yonder Mil.; yá-gi (ya gi) upper = heavenly Mil., opp. to má-gi (ma gi); yar (yar) and yas (yas) v. the respective articles; the word, otherwise, occurs only in compounds: yá-rkan (ya rkan) palate; ya-gád (ya gad) (for skad (skad)) ladder Sch.; ya-góṅ (ya gong) above, over Sch.; ya-mgál (ya mgal), ya-mčú (ya mchu), ya-ṭém (ya them), ya-ṭóg (ya thog), ya-rábs (ya rabs), ya-ré (ya re), ya-só (ya so) v. mgal (mgal) etc.; ya-mṭá (ya mtha') the upper end, i. e. the beginning e. g. of a word, opp. to ma-mṭá (ma mtha') the end Cs.; yá-ṡa (ya sha) esteem, honour, love, shown to a person W. (= że-sa (zhe sa) B., C.), *yá-ṡe (ya she) spé-ra* (*spe ra*) expressions of respect; *yá-ṡe (ya she) pí-la (pi la) zér-na* (*zer na*) if one speaks respectfully; ya-ṡa-méd-ḳan (ya sha med mkhan) uncivil, regardless, reckless, unfeeling; *yá-ṡa (ya sha) ċó-ċe* (*co ce*) to show love, regard, to treat with tenderness, to fondle, a child, animal etc., opp. to má-ṡa (ma sha), which however is less in use.
mutual revilings Ma.: ma (ma) smád-la (smad la) yaṅ (yang) yá-ka (ya ka) sgrags (sgrags) mother and children abuse one another. Cs.: yá-ga (ya ga) bad reputation (?).

(prob. for yya (g.ya) ṅán-pa (ngan pa)) shuddering, fright, anguish, with genit. or accus. of that which is the cause of it Do.; yá-ṅá-bai (ya nga ba'i) dmag-tsógs (dmag tshogs) Mil. a formidable host; yá-ṅai (ya nga'i) gegs (gegs) terrible danger Pth.

prop. ཡ་ཊ་ (ya ṭa) ya-tra (ya tra) (procession and feast, in honour of some idol) W.: festivity, reveling, in beer with dumplings and pastry, held in autumn or winter, in memory and for the benefit of the souls of those that died during the last year.
1. the temples. — 2. a severe cold, catarrh. Med.; *yá-ma (ya ma) rag* (rag) W. I have a bad cold. — 3. n. of a goddess, = brtán-ma (brtan ma).
unsymmetrical, incongruous, not fitting together, e. g. two unequal shoes; of religions, languages,
